memes.label: Miss Abbey Potterson
memes.description: Provides information about Lizzie and the river community

memes.label: Alfred Lammle
memes.description: A "mature young gentleman" of the Veneering circle who marries Sophronia Akershem in a mutual deception over wealth, and becomes entangled in Fledgeby's financial schemes.

memes.label: Appearances and Reality
memes.description: The governing structural tension of Our Mutual Friend — every character, relationship, and social institution operates behind a surface that conceals a different, often contradictory, reality.

memes.label: Bella Wilfer
memes.description: The heroine of Our Mutual Friend, whose journey from mercenary girl to loving woman constitutes the novel's most sustained moral education — learning, through the Boffins' tutelage and Rokesmith's devotion, that love is worth more than money.

memes.label: Bella Wilfer's Moral Transformation
memes.description: Bella Wilfer's journey from a mercenary young woman obsessed with money to a loving wife who chooses love over wealth — orchestrated by Boffin's staged performance as a miser, the novel's most elaborate moral education.

memes.label: Betty Higden
memes.description: A proud, elderly child-minder who embodies Dickens's critique of the Victorian workhouse system and the dignity of the independent poor.

memes.label: Betty Higden's Cottage
memes.description: The humble cottage of Betty Higden — the aged working woman who represents the novel's most powerful argument about the dignity of the independent poor, and whose flight from the workhouse is one of Dickens's most searing critiques of the New Poor Law.

memes.label: Boffin's Bower
memes.description: The residence of Nicodemus and Henrietta Boffin, formerly Harmony Jail, surrounded by the great Harmon dust-heaps at Battle Bridge — the novel's most concentrated image of wealth derived from waste and human generosity reclaiming miserly space.

memes.label: Bradley Headstone
memes.description: The highly certificated schoolmaster of Our Mutual Friend whose suppressed passion for Lizzie Hexam erupts into murderous jealousy, making him one of Dickens's most psychologically complex villains.

memes.label: Brentford Lock
memes.description: The Thames lock at Brentford — a site of passage and transit where Rogue Riderhood attempts to claim a corpse and where the river's role as the novel's great leveller is made most visible.

memes.label: Charley Hexam
memes.description: The brother of Lizzie Hexam in Our Mutual Friend, whose aspirational climb from river-scavenger's son to pupil-teacher represents the moral cost of education divorced from love and loyalty.

memes.label: Covent Garden
memes.description: The London market and entertainment district where the novel's social worlds intersect — the place where the Veneerings go to be seen, where Jenny Wren seeks materials for her dolls' dresses, and where the city's class geography is made most visible through juxtaposition.

memes.label: Crime
memes.description: Crime in Our Mutual Friend as a spectrum rather than a category — from the desperate scavenging of the river people to the cold predation of the Lammles, Dickens asks what distinguishes criminal acts from the ordinary transactions of commercial society.

memes.label: Death and Resurrection
memes.description: The structural and symbolic pattern of death-and-return that shapes the novel's plot — from John Harmon's presumed drowning to the moral resurrections of Bella, Eugene, and the Boffins.

memes.label: Dust
memes.description: The central metaphor of Our Mutual Friend — dust as both literal waste and figurative source of wealth, moral corruption, and social critique in Dickens's final completed novel.

memes.label: Education
memes.description: Education in Our Mutual Friend as a mechanism of class mobility and moral danger — from Charley Hexam's aspirational learning to Bradley Headstone's educated rage.

memes.label: Eugene Wrayburn
memes.description: The brilliant, indolent barrister of Our Mutual Friend whose love for Lizzie Hexam and near-death at Bradley Headstone's hands transform him from amoral observer into morally serious actor.

memes.label: Eugene Wrayburn's Near-Murder
memes.description: Bradley Headstone's violent assault on Eugene Wrayburn at Plashwater Mill — the novel's most brutal scene, in which obsessive jealousy and class resentment erupt into attempted murder, and the river becomes the instrument of both violence and salvation.

memes.label: Reverend Frank Milvey
memes.description: Reads the burial service at Betty Higden's grave

memes.label: Gaffer Hexam
memes.description: Former partner turned accuser; Riderhood falsely denounces Hexam for the Harmon murder

memes.label: Gaffer Hexam's Death
memes.description: The river-scavenger Gaffer Hexam is found dead in the Thames — possibly murdered, possibly drowned — removing Lizzie's father, intensifying the mystery of the Harmon drowning, and shifting the novel's riverside plot from paternal authority to filial independence.

memes.label: George Sampson
memes.description: Known to the Wilfer household as a family friend

memes.label: Georgiana Podsnap
memes.description: Pursued by Fledgeby as a prospective wife under the Lammles' scheme

memes.label: Greed
memes.description: The predatory impulse driving characters across the social spectrum in Our Mutual Friend — from Wegg's dust-mound scheming to the Lammles' marriage of mutual deception.

memes.label: Greenwich
memes.description: A Thames-side town in southeast London, associated with riverside dining, the church where Bella Wilfer and John Rokesmith are married, and excursions in Our Mutual Friend.

memes.label: Bradley Headstone's Proposal Rejected
memes.description: Bradley Headstone's desperate marriage proposal to Lizzie Hexam is rejected — the rejection that transforms his obsessive "love" into murderous rage and sets the education plot on its dark trajectory toward violence.

memes.label: Hexam Riverside Dwelling
memes.description: The poorest domestic space in Our Mutual Friend — the riverside dwelling where Gaffer Hexam raised Lizzie and Charley, and where the novel's most intimate scenes of working-class family life unfold against the constant presence of the Thames.

memes.label: Holloway
memes.description: A northern London suburb, home to the Wilfer family in Our Mutual Friend — the modest domestic setting from which Bella Wilfer's story unfolds.

memes.label: Identity and Disguise
memes.description: The pattern of hidden, doubled, and transformed identities that structures Our Mutual Friend — from Harmon's Rokesmith disguise to the performative selves of Victorian society.

memes.label: Inheritance
memes.description: Inheritance as the novel's master plot mechanism — wills that control from beyond the grave, fortunes that test the living, and the question of what we owe to those who came before.

memes.label: Jenny Wren
memes.description: The crippled dolls' dressmaker of Our Mutual Friend, whose sharp tongue, visionary imagination, and fierce independence make her one of Dickens's most original creations — a child-woman who sees through the pretensions of the adult world.

memes.label: Jenny Wren's Workshop
memes.description: The small room in Church Street where Jenny Wren — the "dolls' dressmaker" — creates her miniature garments from the discarded materials of fashionable London, transforming waste into art and pain into beauty.

memes.label: Jenny Wren's Cry
memes.description: Jenny Wren's outburst — "Come up and be dead!" — the dolls' dressmaker's characteristic inversion of power relations, in which the disabled child commands the able-bodied adults around her.

memes.label: John Harmon / John Rokesmith
memes.description: The protagonist of Our Mutual Friend, presumed drowned on his return to England, who assumes the identity of John Rokesmith to observe how those around him behave when they believe the heir is dead.

memes.label: John Harmon's Presumed Drowning
memes.description: The novel's initiating event — John Harmon, returning to claim his inheritance, is presumed murdered and his body found in the Thames, setting in motion the plot's central mystery of identity and inheritance.

memes.label: Johnny Higden
memes.description: The orphaned great-grandson of Betty Higden, whose illness and death at the Children's Hospital form an emotionally significant subplot in the novel.

memes.label: Justice and the Law
memes.description: The gap between legal process and moral justice in Our Mutual Friend — a novel populated by lawyers, wills, and courtroom drama that systematically questions whether the law serves justice.

memes.label: Lady Tippins
memes.description: Serves as a grotesque chorus commenting on Eugene's romantic entanglements

memes.label: Lavinia Wilfer
memes.description: The object of George Sampson's affections after Bella rejects him

memes.label: Limehouse
memes.description: The East London riverside district that serves as the novel's primary setting for its working-class characters — the home of the Hexams, Rogue Riderhood, and the Six Jolly Fellowship Porters, and the geographical counterpoint to the West End's fashionable squares.

memes.label: Limehouse Hole
memes.description: A riverside slum in East London, home to Rogue Riderhood, where the Thames-side working poor and criminal underclass dwell "deep and dark" among the riggers and boat-builders.

memes.label: Lizzie Hexam
memes.description: The daughter of the river-scavenger Gaffer Hexam, whose moral clarity, self-sacrifice, and capacity for love make her the novel's most admirable character — and whose cross-class marriage to Eugene Wrayburn represents Dickens's most radical union.

memes.label: London Bridge
memes.description: The historic stone bridge spanning the Thames, appearing in the novel's opening scene and serving as a key landmark in the geography of Our Mutual Friend.

memes.label: Lord Snigsworth
memes.description: Fledgeby dreads Snigsworth's power to "taboo and prohibit"

memes.label: Marriage
memes.description: Marriage in Our Mutual Friend as a financial contract, a moral test, and a site of Dickens's most radical critique of how money corrupts the most intimate human bond.

memes.label: Mincing Lane
memes.description: The commercial street in the City of London that represents the novel's world of trade, finance, and legal machinery — the district where the Harmon fortune was accumulated and where "the law" makes its claims on property and inheritance.

memes.label: Miss Peecher
memes.description: Secretly in love with Headstone, her fellow educator; he barely notices her feelings

memes.label: Money
memes.description: The driving force of plot and character in Our Mutual Friend — money as the measure of all human relations, from the Harmon inheritance to Bella Wilfer's moral education.

memes.label: Morality
memes.description: The moral framework of Our Mutual Friend — a secular ethics in which character is revealed by money, love is the highest virtue, and moral growth requires the active discernment of surfaces from substance.

memes.label: Mortimer Lightwood
memes.description: The solicitor and "man from the law" of Our Mutual Friend, whose dry wit, professional nonchalance, and friendship with Eugene Wrayburn make him the novel's principal observer-narrator within the world of the story.

memes.label: Mr. Aaron
memes.description: Serves Fledgeby's financial interests

memes.label: Mr. Dolls
memes.description: Eugene treats Mr. Dolls with a kind of clinical amusement, "winding him up" with rum

memes.label: Mr. Fledgeby
memes.description: Harassed by Jenny Wren, who sees through his pretensions and rebukes him sharply

memes.label: Mr. Inspector
memes.description: Investigates the presumed death that sets the novel's plot in motion

memes.label: Mr. Podsnap
memes.description: Views the lawyer Lightwood with suspicion for his ironic detachment from Podsnappery

memes.label: Mr. Riah
memes.description: Provides refuge and employment to Lizzie Hexam when she is in distress

memes.label: Mr. Sloppy
memes.description: Taken into the Boffin household after Betty Higden's death

memes.label: Mr. Twemlow
memes.description: Regards Wrayburn with a mixture of gentlemanly courtesy and moral unease

memes.label: Mr. Venus
memes.description: The taxidermist and "articulator of human bones" in Our Mutual Friend, whose shop of preserved specimens provides the novel's most grotesque comic setting and whose reluctant partnership with Silas Wegg drives the dust-plot counter-conspiracy.

memes.label: Mr. Venus's Shop
memes.description: The taxidermy establishment of Mr. Venus in Clerkenwell, a space of preserved specimens and articulated skeletons that serves as the novel's grotesque laboratory for examining the relationship between life and death, value and waste, parts and wholes.

memes.label: Mrs. Boffin
memes.description: Takes in the orphan Sloppy after Betty Higden's death

memes.label: Mrs. Lammle
memes.description: Attempts to manipulate the naive Georgiana into marriage with Fledgeby

memes.label: Mrs. Podsnap
memes.description: Mother of Georgiana; participates in the social management of her daughter

memes.label: Mrs. Wilfer
memes.description: The "majestic" matriarch of the Wilfer household in Our Mutual Friend, whose towering dignity, rhetorical grandeur, and unshakeable conviction of her own superiority provide some of the novel's most sustained domestic comedy.

memes.label: Nicodemus Boffin
memes.description: The "Golden Dustman" of Our Mutual Friend — a former servant who inherits the Harmon dust fortune, whose apparent moral decline into miserliness is revealed as a deliberate performance to teach Bella Wilfer the true value of love over money.

memes.label: Old Harmon
memes.description: Employed Boffin as his foreman; Boffin inherits the Harmon fortune as secondary heir

memes.label: Plashwater Mill
memes.description: The remote lock-keeper's cottage on the upper Thames where Bradley Headstone attacks Eugene Wrayburn — the novel's most violent scene, in which the river's pastoral surface is shattered by the dark currents of obsession and class resentment.

memes.label: Pleasant Riderhood
memes.description: The daughter of Rogue Riderhood in Our Mutual Friend, whose name is the novel's cruelest joke and whose hard-won "little position" as a pawnbroker's assistant represents one of Dickens's most quietly determined female characters.

memes.label: Pleasant Riderhood's Proposal
memes.description: Mr. Venus's improbable marriage proposal to Pleasant Riderhood, delivered in his shop of preserved specimens — the novel's oddest romantic scene and its most democratic vision of love.

memes.label: Podsnap's House
memes.description: "The home of John Podsnap, a bulky man of unshakeable self-satisfaction, whose domestic rituals of bourgeois complacency represent the novel's most concentrated attack on the Victorian cult of respectability."

memes.label: Poverty
memes.description: Poverty in Our Mutual Friend as both material condition and moral test — from Betty Higden's defiant independence to the respectable Veneerings' hollow prosperity.

memes.label: R. Wilfer
memes.description: The shabby-genteel clerk and father of Bella Wilfer, known throughout Our Mutual Friend as "the Cherub" for his round, rosy face and cheerful resignation to a life of domestic subordination.

memes.label: River Thames
memes.description: The major river flowing through London, serving as the novel's opening setting and a recurring symbol of death, trade, and the passage of life in Our Mutual Friend.

memes.label: Rogue Riderhood
memes.description: The amoral river-scavenger and informant of Our Mutual Friend, whose greed, cunning, and capacity for survival make him the novel's most relentless predator of the Thames-side world.

memes.label: Rogue Riderhood's Drowning
memes.description: Rogue Riderhood's death by drowning in the Thames — killed by the river he exploited throughout his life — in a failed attempt to murder Bradley Headstone, representing the novel's most complete act of poetic justice.

memes.label: Silas Wegg
memes.description: The one-legged ballad-monger and "literary man" of Our Mutual Friend, who schemes to blackmail the Boffins over a second will in pursuit of the Harmon fortune.

memes.label: Silas Wegg's Counter-Conspiracy
memes.description: Silas Wegg's scheme to blackmail Boffin by searching the dust-heaps for a hidden will — the novel's great comic subplot, in which venality, ignorance, and greed conspire against the incorruptible Golden Dustman and inevitably fail.

memes.label: The Six Jolly Fellowship Porters
memes.description: The riverside tavern in Limehouse presided over by Abbey Potterson, serving as the novel's central gathering place for the working poor of the Thames — a space of rough honesty and moral clarity that stands in pointed contrast to the fashionable dinner tables of the Veneering circle.

memes.label: Sloppy's Reading
memes.description: The foundling Sloppy, raised by Betty Higden, demonstrates his unexpected gift for reading aloud — the novel's quietest argument that talent and intelligence are independent of class.

memes.label: Smithfield Market
memes.description: The great London livestock market — a place of brutal commerce that represents the city's unvarnished material economy of flesh and money, and the gross counterpoint to the Veneering table's refined consumption.

memes.label: Social Class
memes.description: Dickens's anatomy of Victorian class structure in Our Mutual Friend — from the dust-mounds to the Veneering dinner table, a society organised by money rather than birth.

memes.label: The Boffin Household
memes.description: The domestic establishment of Nicodemus and Henrietta Boffin at Boffin's Bower — the novel's model of wealth used well, where inherited fortune expresses rather than corrupts the inheritors' decency, and where identity is tested through service rather than asserted through performance.

memes.label: The Boffins' Inheritance
memes.description: Nicodemus and Henrietta Boffin, the loyal servants of Old Harmon, inherit the entire Harmon fortune upon John Harmon's presumed death — transforming them from working-class domestics to wealthy householders and upending the novel's social order.

memes.label: The Boffins' Poultry Yard
memes.description: The transformation of Harmony Jail's poultry yard into a space of domestic abundance — the novel's quietest image of how wealth, in the right hands, creates life rather than hoarding it.

memes.label: The Harmon Will Reading
memes.description: The reading of Old Harmon's will — the legal event that sets the novel's inheritance plot in motion, in which the dead man's perversity structures the lives of the living.

memes.label: The Hexam Family
memes.description: The riverside family of Gaffer, Lizzie, and Charley Hexam — the novel's primary working-class family unit, whose dynamics of loyalty, shame, and betrayal embody the novel's argument about class and moral worth.

memes.label: The Pauper School
memes.description: The educational institution that produces both Bradley Headstone and Charley Hexam — the novel's indictment of a system that teaches boys to parse Latin but not to honor their sisters.

memes.label: The River Thames
memes.description: The Thames as the novel's liquid unconscious — a site of death, salvage, moral ambiguity, and hidden truth that mirrors and opposes the dust-mounds on land.

memes.label: The Riverside Community
memes.description: The informal community of watermen, lightermen, and their families centered on the Six Jolly Fellowship Porters — the novel's most fully realized working-class collective, governed by Abbey Potterson's moral authority.

memes.label: The Temple
memes.description: The ancient legal precinct in London where Mortimer Lightwood and Eugene Wrayburn maintain chambers, representing the professional world of law that stands in ironic contrast to the novel's actual mechanisms of justice.

memes.label: The Veneering Circle
memes.description: The social network assembled around Mr. and Mrs. Veneering of Holloway — the novel's definitive portrait of fashionable society as a marketplace of mutual display, where "oldest friends" are acquaintances of a few months and every relationship is a transaction awaiting its settlement date.

memes.label: The Veneering Household
memes.description: "The fashionable residence of Mr. and Mrs. Veneering, bran-new people in a bran-new house in a bran-new quarter of London, whose dinner table is the novel's primary stage for its satire of social climbing, hollow sociability, and the performance of status."

memes.label: The Veneerings' Dinner Party
memes.description: The Veneerings' dinner party — the novel's great set-piece of social satire, where "bran-new people" assemble "bran-new friends" around a "bran-new table" in a ritual of hollow sociability that defines the novel's critique of fashionable London.

memes.label: The Veneerings' Social Collapse
memes.description: The Veneerings' financial ruin and social abandonment — the novel's verdict on a society that values appearance over substance, in which "friends" assembled for display disappear with mechanical efficiency when the display can no longer be funded.
aliases: the collapse of the bran-new world

memes.label: Westminster
memes.description: The political and legal heart of London on the Thames north bank, referenced in Our Mutual Friend for its institutions of law, government, and royal ceremony.
